roleBasis: Last author of the Crossref author list of 73, verified on 2026-07-23. The contribution statement was not re-read in this pass.
continuity: Extends the laboratory's question about what GPX4 actually requires, by separating catalytic capacity from membrane access rather than adding another GPX4 substitute.

roleBasis: First author with Stuart L. Schreiber as last author, verified at Crossref on 2026-07-23. The work therefore predates the independent Westlake laboratory and must not be presented as that laboratory leading it.
continuity: Origin of the laboratory's later question about how lipid composition and redox flux set tissue-specific sensitivity.

roleBasis: First author with Stuart L. Schreiber as last author, verified at Crossref on 2026-07-23.
continuity: Establishes the ether-lipid substrate pool that the independent laboratory now studies as a measurable and manipulable cell state.

roleBasis: Last author of the Crossref author list of 32, verified on 2026-07-23.
continuity: The founding answer to the laboratory's persistent question: a second membrane-antioxidant system that operates without glutathione.

roleBasis: Edward William Tate is author 24 of 32 in the verified Crossref author list, a middle position. The project audit archive records this contribution as chemical-biology support and explicitly not laboratory leadership.
continuity: Connects the laboratory's chemical-proteomics capability to the identification of a myristoylation-dependent ferroptosis suppressor.

roleBasis: Derek A. Pratt is author 30 of 32 in the verified Crossref author list. The contribution statement has not been checked, so no leadership or method-ownership claim is made here.
continuity: Relevant to the laboratory's question about radical-trapping rates, but the specific contribution to this paper remains unverified.

roleBasis: Last author of the Crossref author list of 27, verified on 2026-07-23.
continuity: Widens the same persistent question from coenzyme Q to a second quinone class handled by the same reductase.

roleBasis: First author with Marcus Conrad as last author, verified at Crossref on 2026-07-23. The work was carried out before the independent Tohoku appointment and is not presented as that laboratory leading it.
continuity: Sets up the independent laboratory's line on quinone cycles and selenium utilisation beyond GPX4.

roleBasis: First author of the Crossref author list of 26, verified on 2026-07-23, in a paper whose redox-lipidomics platform is the laboratory's own.
continuity: The defining statement of the laboratory's persistent question: which oxidised phospholipid species drive death rather than accompany it.

roleBasis: Last author of the verified Crossref author list of 26, checked on 2026-07-23.
continuity: Same species-level question, later carried into brain injury as the laboratory's independent line.

roleBasis: Last author of the Crossref author list of 46, verified on 2026-07-23.
continuity: Directly answers the laboratory's question by converting lysosomal iron in resistant cell states into a designed chemical vulnerability.

roleBasis: Last author of the Crossref author list of 9, verified on 2026-07-23.
continuity: Moves the laboratory's membrane-damage question from rupture within one cell to transfer between adjacent cells.

roleBasis: Last author of the Crossref author list of 28, verified on 2026-07-23.
continuity: Tests the laboratory's stated question directly: whether inhibition confined to the perfusion circuit improves graft function without exposing a recipient.

roleBasis: Last author of the Crossref author list of 47, verified on 2026-07-23.
continuity: Addresses the sex half of the laboratory's question, while the propagation half remains supported mainly by synchronised tubular imaging.

roleBasis: Last author of the Crossref author list of 13, verified on 2026-07-23.
continuity: The mitochondrial arm of the laboratory's question, and the case where a formal Matters Arising keeps the pharmacological attribution open.

roleBasis: Last author of the 15-author Crossref author list (Brent R. Stockwell), verified at Crossref on 2026-07-24. The contribution statement was not re-read in this pass.
continuity: Extends the laboratory's programme on where in the cell lethal lipid peroxidation is initiated and how ferroptosis can be chemically probed and blocked.

roleBasis: Last author of the 27-author Crossref author list (Huiyong Yin), verified at Crossref on 2026-07-24. The contribution statement was not re-read in this pass.
continuity: Extends the laboratory's work on oxidized-lipid mass spectrometry and how lipid oxidation drives cardiovascular ferroptosis and ischemic injury.

roleBasis: Last author of the 20-author Crossref author list (Thales Papagiannakopoulos; Marcus Conrad is second-to-last), verified at Crossref on 2026-07-24. The contribution statement was not re-read in this pass.
continuity: Extends the laboratory's work on how the in vivo tumour environment and redox metabolism set lung-cancer vulnerabilities, here an FSP1-dependent ferroptosis.

roleBasis: Last author of the 32-author Crossref author list (Jessalyn M. Ubellacker), verified at Crossref on 2026-07-24. The contribution statement was not re-read in this pass.
continuity: Extends the laboratory's work on how the metastatic environment, and the lymph node in particular, reshapes oxidative stress and ferroptosis susceptibility.

roleBasis: Last author of the 17-author Crossref author list (Ken-ichi Yamada), verified at Crossref on 2026-07-24. The contribution statement was not re-read in this pass.
continuity: Extends the laboratory's work on radical-trapping probes and where in the cell lipid peroxidation is initiated and propagated during ferroptosis.

roleBasis: Last author of the 16-author Crossref author list (James A. Olzmann), verified at Crossref on 2026-07-24. The contribution statement was not re-read in this pass.
continuity: Extends the laboratory's work on lipid-droplet biology and how neutral-lipid quality control intersects with ferroptosis and membrane lipid oxidation.

roleBasis: Second-to-last author of the 16-author Crossref author list (Maria Fedorova), a co-senior position with last author James A. Olzmann, verified at Crossref on 2026-07-24. The contribution statement was not re-read in this pass.
continuity: Extends the laboratory's redox-lipidomics and epilipidomics work on oxidized lipid species, here mapping oxidized neutral lipids under FSP1 loss.

roleBasis: Author 5 of the 10-author Crossref author list (Hirotaka Imai), a contributing co-author; the senior last author is Takashi Ueta. Position verified at Crossref on 2026-07-24, and the contribution statement was not re-read in this pass.
continuity: Connects to the laboratory's long work on phospholipid hydroperoxide GPX4 biology, here contributed to an RPE-specific GPx4-loss retinal degeneration model.

roleBasis: Second-to-last author of the 25-author Crossref author list (Eikan Mishima), a co-senior position with last author Marcus Conrad. Position verified at Crossref on 2026-07-24, and the contribution statement was not re-read in this pass.
continuity: Extends the laboratory's work on selenium and selenoprotein biology in ferroptosis, here defining PRDX6 as a selenium carrier that supports GPX4 synthesis.

roleBasis: Last author of the 10-author Crossref author list (Xuejun Jiang), verified at Crossref on 2026-07-24. The contribution statement was not re-read in this pass.
continuity: Extends the laboratory's work on GPX4-independent ferroptosis surveillance and how membrane lipid composition sets the ferroptosis threshold.

roleBasis: Last author of the 12-author Crossref author list (Scott J. Dixon), verified at Crossref on 2026-07-24. The contribution statement was not re-read in this pass.
continuity: Extends the laboratory's work on the lipid determinants of ferroptosis, here how exogenous monounsaturated fatty acids remodel membranes into a resistant state.

roleBasis: First author of the 7-author Crossref author list (Minghui Gao), with senior last author Xuejun Jiang, verified at Crossref on 2026-07-24. The work predates the independent Harbin Institute of Technology laboratory and must not be presented as that laboratory leading it.
continuity: Origin of the laboratory's later work on the metabolic and mitochondrial control of ferroptosis, done as first author before the independent laboratory.

roleBasis: Last author of the 30-author Crossref author list (Weiping Zou), verified at Crossref on 2026-07-24. The contribution statement was not re-read in this pass.
continuity: Extends the laboratory's tumour-immunology work, here linking CD8+ T-cell IFN-gamma to tumour ferroptosis sensitivity during checkpoint immunotherapy.

roleBasis: Last author of the 7-author Crossref author list (Derek A. Pratt), verified at Crossref on 2026-07-24. The contribution statement was not re-read in this pass.
continuity: Extends the laboratory's radical-trapping-antioxidant chemistry, here defining how ferrostatin-1 and liproxstatin-1 act in the membrane rather than on 15-LOX.

roleBasis: First author of the 13-author Crossref author list (Ken-ichi Yamada) and originator of the NBD-Pen lipid-radical probe that defines this laboratory's method line; the last author is Mayumi Yamato, the senior radical-detection collaborator. Crossref marks no corresponding author; positions verified 2026-07-24. Attributed as co-lead rather than sole lead because seniority is shared and not machine-verifiable here.
continuity: Origin of the laboratory's radical-trapping fluorescence-probe methods later used in its lysosomal ferroptosis work, here first developed for lipid-derived radicals.
